reforms.description: The Swiss Parliament has approved CHF120 million in funding for the second phase (2017-20) of the capacity building of the Swiss Competence Centres for Energy Research, which are R&D clusters grouping academia and industry to focus on eight energy fields including electricity generation and storage and biomass.
reforms.package_name: Swiss Competence Centres for Energy Research
reforms.component_name: Swiss Competence Centres for Energy Research
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The funding aims to enhance R&D capabilities in energy sectors, which is expected to drive innovation and productivity.
reforms.status_evidence: The funding has been approved and is currently in effect.
reforms.importance_rationale: The funding represents a significant investment in R&D capacity but is not a structural overhaul.
reforms.source_quote: The Swiss Parliament has approved CHF120 million in funding for the second phase (2017-20) of the capacity building of the Swiss Competence Centres for Energy Research.

reforms.description: The Federal Council adopted the 'New Growth Policy 2016-19', which includes measures to promote productivity growth and resilience in the economy.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The policy aims to enhance productivity and resilience, which are crucial for long-term economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The policy has been adopted and is currently in effect.
reforms.importance_rationale: The policy represents a structural change in how the government approaches economic growth and innovation.
reforms.source_quote: In June 2017 the Federal Council adopted its 'New Growth Policy 2016-19', including by creating a productivity commission.

reforms.description: Switzerland is implementing a corporate tax reform that aims to reinforce its position as an R&D hub by providing R&D tax incentives for all firms, including domestic ones, and may include a 'patent box' to offer reduced tax rates on patent revenues.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The tax incentives are designed to encourage firms to invest more in R&D, which is expected to enhance innovation and productivity.
reforms.status_evidence: The reform has been proposed and is awaiting implementation.
reforms.importance_rationale: The reform represents a significant overhaul of the corporate tax system aimed at stimulating R&D investment.
reforms.source_quote: The ongoing corporate tax reform is an opportunity to reinforce the Swiss position as an R&D hub with R&D tax incentives available for all firms including domestic ones.

reforms.description: Switzerland aims to boost entry by innovative start-ups to reverse the productivity divergence between frontier firms and the rest of the economy, including promoting incubators at higher education institutions to enhance the creation and success of start-ups.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Enhancing the startup ecosystem is expected to drive innovation and economic growth by fostering new business creation.
reforms.status_evidence: The initiatives are currently under discussion and have not yet been implemented.
reforms.importance_rationale: The initiatives represent significant efforts to improve the startup environment but do not constitute a major structural reform.
reforms.source_quote: Promote incubators at higher education institutions, and recruit academic staff with entrepreneurial skills to boost start-ups’ creation and success.

reforms.description: The New Regional Policy, renewed in 2016, aims to boost innovation and competitiveness in lagging areas, focusing on rural, mountainous, and border regions under the European Territorial Co-operation programme.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The policy aims to enhance innovation and competitiveness in underdeveloped regions, which can contribute to overall economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The policy has been renewed and is currently in effect.
reforms.importance_rationale: The renewal of the policy represents a significant effort to address regional disparities but is not a major overhaul.
reforms.source_quote: The New Regional Policy, which entered into force in 2008 and was renewed in 2016, aims at boosting lagging areas.

reforms.description: The Federal Council is considering various measures to encourage innovation, including tax incentives for business angels and amendments to the insolvency regime to provide additional options for restructuring distressed companies.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: These measures are aimed at fostering a more conducive environment for innovation and entrepreneurship.
reforms.status_evidence: The measures are currently under consideration and have not yet been implemented.
reforms.importance_rationale: While these measures are significant, they do not represent a major structural change.
reforms.source_quote: A welcome amendment is currently being discussed in parliament: it would propose additional options for restructuring distressed companies, including creating incentives to take actions at an early stage and avoid insolvency.

reforms.description: The Swiss government has implemented a five-year fund to expand childcare services, aimed at better matching parents' working hours and addressing supply-demand tensions in childcare availability, alongside the Continuing Education and Training Act to enhance funding and coordination for continuing education outside formal higher education.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Improving access to childcare and enhancing continuing education are expected to support workforce participation and skill development.
reforms.status_evidence: Both initiatives have been implemented and are currently in effect.
reforms.importance_rationale: These initiatives represent a significant investment in human capital development.
reforms.source_quote: The government has established and replenished a fund to expand childcare provision; to be effective the additional places should be located in areas of high demand and better cover parents’ working hours.
